summary: Validated Fast2SMS pathway, user notifications inbox APIs/UI, renewal dispatch endpoint/UI, assignment notification behavior, and core regressions (auth, booking, subscriptions, admin, track-booking).

minor.issue: Fast2SMS success logic treats any HTTP <300 as success even if provider response indicates return=false; this can produce false-positive SMS success logs.

action_items: Update _send_sms_message success condition to rely on Fast2SMS response semantics (e.g., return/status_code from payload), not only HTTP status.
After fixing SMS success semantics, retest assignment + booking + renewal reminder notification logs for accurate success/failure reporting.
critical_code_review_comments: _send_sms_message currently uses: success = bool(response_data.get('return', False)) or response.status_code < 300; this can mark failed Fast2SMS sends as successful when API returns HTTP 200 with failure payload.
Synchronous requests.post is still used in async-driven notification/payment codepaths; this can reduce throughput under high concurrency.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST/UI users, bookings, worker subscriptions, worker signup records, and admin booking updates through API/UI tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: This run validated /api/users/notifications GET + PATCH read, /api/admin/subscriptions/dispatch-renewal-reminders (200 shape), assignment update inbox entries, UI /account/notifications mark-read flow, and admin renewal dispatch button. Fast2SMS path was exercised and produced Fast2SMS-tagged SMS log details.
rca of the issue: Root cause for potential SMS reporting inaccuracy: _send_sms_message sets success true when HTTP status <300 even if Fast2SMS payload indicates failure. Repro by forcing provider to return business-level failure with HTTP 200 and checking notification_log.success/detail mismatch. Mitigation: parse and enforce provider-level success indicators first, then map failure reason into detail.
